places.name: Cogito Coffee
places.description: Award-winning specialty roaster and café known for meticulous sourcing and modern espresso bar craft.
places.local_story: Cogito helped define third-wave coffee in Croatia and still feels like the benchmark when locals argue about the best cup in town. Regulars treat it as a pit stop between errands or a quiet place to work through a single pour-over for half an hour.
places.practical_tip: Ask what is on filter that day; pair a pour-over with their pastry if available.

places.name: Eliscaffe
places.description: Tiny Ilica landmark run by Croatia's most famous coffee personality, serving obsessively crafted espresso in a standing-room-only bar.
places.local_story: Nikola Orešković turned this sliver of Ilica into a pilgrimage site for coffee people long before specialty shops multiplied. Locals squeeze in for a quick kava that is anything but quick in ambition — it is theater, ritual, and craft in a few square meters.
places.practical_tip: Order espresso or macchiato, watch the bar workflow, and do not rush the barista; peak times mean a short wait is normal.

places.name: Botaničar
places.description: Stylish café-bar steps from the Botanical Garden, mixing coffee, cocktails, and a steady calendar of small cultural events.
places.local_story: Students and creatives use Botaničar as a bridge between the greenhouses and the city grid — coffee in the afternoon often turns into something louder after dark. It is less about quiet laptop focus and more about seeing who is in town.
places.practical_tip: Grab coffee before or after a stroll in the garden; evenings shift to drinks — check the day’s opening if you want a calm cup.

places.name: Express Bar
places.description: Beloved specialty coffee bar on Petrinjska with direct-trade beans, skilled latte art, and a deep, window-heavy perch above the downtown flow.
places.local_story: The name nods to old Zagreb café history while the menu is fully modern — locals meet here before work or during špica when the street outside becomes a parade. The window seat is prime real estate for watching the city argue with itself in passing.
places.practical_tip: Try a flat white or filter; snag a window spot for Petrinjska people-watching.

places.name: Caffe Bar Finjak
places.description: Courtyard kafić on Vlaška known for vintage décor, a classic Faema machine, and unapologetically old-school charm.
places.local_story: Finjak is where Instagram meets generations of kafić habit — neighbors still treat it as their living room while newcomers hunt the courtyard entrance. The vibe is pure Zagreb: loud, warm, and slightly theatrical.
places.practical_tip: Order a classic espresso or macchiato; explore the courtyard seating and take in the retro interior details.

places.name: Velvet
places.description: Elegant café on Dežmanova near Zrinjevac, known for brunch, cakes, and a polished room that feels like a small grand café.
places.local_story: Velvet sits where locals transition from park walks to city business — coffee comes with cake culture and long, unhurried tables. On weekends it is a špica-adjacent scene without the full square crush.
places.practical_tip: Pair coffee with a slice of cake or a brunch plate; reserve or arrive early on weekends for a terrace-adjacent seat in season.
